methods.name: _get_filesync_confidence
methods.docstring:
#[Mark:P1-DynamicConfidence] Data-driven confidence scoring using manifest + Filesync + Os_Toolkit

Args:
    action_id: Action identifier
    action_context: Context with: scattered_plan_count, security_anomalies, todo_conflicts, file_path, etc.

Returns:
    Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on ACTUAL system state

Data sources:
1. Latest Filesync manifest: babel_data/timeline/manifests/manifest_latest.json
2. Filesync project associations: manifest['files'][file_id]['project_association']
3. Os_Toolkit artifact properties: self.session.artifacts[artifact_id].properties
4. Timeline correlation: Compare modified_time across related files

zenity_forms.docstring:
Display Zenity forms dialog for structured input.

Args:
    fields: List of dicts with {'name': 'field_name', 'label': 'Display Label', 'default': 'value'}
    title: Window title

Returns:
    Dict mapping field names to user input, or None if cancelled

Example:
    result = zenity_forms([
        {'name': 'task_id', 'label': 'Task ID', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'title', 'label': 'Title', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'priority', 'label': 'Priority', 'default': 'P2'}
    ], title="Add Todo")

zenity_checklist.docstring:
Display Zenity checklist for multi-select.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'checked': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    List of selected item IDs

Example:
    selected = zenity_checklist([
        {'id': 'P0-1', 'label': 'Remove Gemini from SYSTEM_DNA', 'checked': True},
        {'id': 'P1-2', 'label': 'Add log integration', 'checked': False}
    ], title="Select Tasks", text="Which tasks to work on?")

zenity_progress.docstring:
Start Zenity progress bar (returns Popen object for updating).

    Args:
        title: Window title
        text: Description text
        pulsate: If True, show activity indicator; if False, expect percentage updates

    Returns:
        Popen object - send "PERCENTAGE
" to stdin to update, close stdin when done

    Example:
        progress = zenity_progress(title="Scanning", text="Analyzing files...")
        for i in range(0, 101, 10):
            progress.stdin.write(f"{i}
".encode())
            progress.stdin.flush()
            time.sleep(0.5)
        progress.stdin.close()
        progress.wait()

zenity_notification.docstring:
Display desktop notification.

Args:
    message: Notification text
    urgency: "low" | "normal" | "critical"

Example:
    zenity_notification("Security check complete - 3 warnings found", urgency="critical")

zenity_question.docstring:
Display yes/no question dialog.

Args:
    text: Question to ask
    title: Window title
    ok_label: Label for confirm button
    cancel_label: Label for cancel button

Returns:
    True if user clicked Yes/OK, False otherwise

Example:
    if zenity_question("Run suggested command: git status?", title="Execute?"):
        subprocess.run(['git', 'status'])

zenity_radiolist.docstring:
Display Zenity radiolist for single-select from options.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'selected': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    Selected item ID or None if cancelled

Example:
    action = zenity_radiolist([
        {'id': 'run_tests', 'label': 'Run test suite', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_security', 'label': 'Security scan', 'selected': False},
        {'id': 'sync_todos', 'label': 'Sync all todos', 'selected': False}
    ], title="Suggested Actions", text="What would you like to do next?")

display_output_with_actions.docstring:
Enhanced display that shows content then prompts for action selection.

Args:
    content: Main output text to display
    actions: List of suggested actions with {'id', 'label', 'command', 'selected'}
    title: Window title
    use_zenity: If True, use Zenity GUI; otherwise print to stdout

Returns:
    Selected action ID, or None if no action chosen

Workflow:
    1. Display content (text-info or stdout)
    2. Show action radiolist (if actions provided)
    3. Return selected action ID for caller to execute

Example:
    actions = [
        {'id': 'grep_imports', 'label': 'Find all imports in this file',
         'command': 'grep "^import\|^from" Os_Toolkit.py', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_todos', 'label': 'Show related todos',
         'command': 'python3 Os_Toolkit.py todo view --grep Os_Toolkit', 'selected': False}
    ]

    action_id = display_output_with_actions(
        query_results,
        actions,
        title="Query Results: Os_Toolkit.py",
        use_zenity=True
    )

    if action_id:
        action = next(a for a in actions if a['id'] == action_id)
        subprocess.run(action['command'], shell=True)

load_version_manifest.docstring: Loads the version manifest from disk.

save_version_manifest.docstring:
Saves the version manifest to disk using an atomic write (temp → rename)
to prevent file corruption from concurrent writes by the live watcher and UI.
#[Mark:ATOMIC_MANIFEST_SAVE]

register_new_version.docstring: Registers a new version in the manifest.

mark_version_status.docstring:
Updates the status of a version (stable, unstable, damaged).
Uses robust error handling to prevent recovery system crashes.

set_default_version.docstring: Sets a specific version as the system default for rollbacks.

get_version_info.docstring: Returns metadata for a specific version.

check_last_launch_status.docstring:
Determines if the previous session succeeded using manifest data and log markers.
Distinguishes between:
  - 'stable': launched successfully, all tabs init'd
  - 'unstable': launched but some tabs failed to init
  - 'damaged': never reached mainloop (crash before LAUNCH_SUCCESS)
#[Mark:VERSION_STATUS_CHECK]

get_blame_for_file.docstring: Retrieves all crash history associated with a specific file.

find_latest_version.docstring: Returns the most recent version timestamp from the manifest.

get_feature_for_path.docstring: Maps a relative file path to a high-level application feature.

test_phase_5_enrichment.docstring: Test marker for Phase 5.1 enriched attribution output in live watcher.

register_event.docstring:
Registers an event in the version manifest and creates association pools.
#[Mark:EVENT_SEQUENCING_REGISTRATION]

Stores:
- version_manifest['events'][event_id] = event data
- version_manifest['event_pools'][feature_id] = [list of event_ids]
- version_manifest['event_timeline'] = chronological list of events

assess_change_risk.docstring:
Analyzes a change to flag potentially breaking modifications.
#[Mark:RISKY_CHANGE_DETECTION]

Returns dict: {
    risk_level: LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH|CRITICAL,
    confidence: 0.0-1.0,
    reasons: [list of triggered rules],
    file_criticality: core|important|normal,
    suggested_undo: bool
}

record_version_crash.docstring: Stores crash context, generates a unique Bug ID, and tracks its occurrences.

find_all_good_versions.docstring:
Scans all log files in the DeBug directory to find all successful launch timestamps.

Returns:
    list: A sorted list of version timestamps (most recent first).

get_version_changes.docstring:
Identifies files that changed 'in' a specific version.
A file belongs to version V if its last_modified time is > the version before V
and <= V.

Args:
    version_ts (str): The target version timestamp.
    all_versions (list): Sorted list of all version timestamps.

Returns:
    list: Relative paths of files that changed in this version.

restore_project_to_version.docstring:
Restores all project files to their state at a specific version timestamp.

Args:
    version_timestamp (str): The timestamp to restore to.
    
Returns:
    tuple: (bool, str) Success status and summary message.

attempt_automatic_recovery.docstring:
Main entry point for the recovery process. Called on critical failure.
Offers rollback to 'Default' version if set, otherwise last good version.

methods.name: refresh_latest_report
methods.docstring:
Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

methods.name: sync_todos

methods.name: consolidate_plans
methods.docstring:
Discover scattered .md plan files with #[Mark:] annotations.

Scans a user-chosen directory (or Trainer/Data/ by default).
Shows a dialog to review found files. On confirm:
  1. Extracts #[Mark:] lines from each file
  2. Offers to copy (not move) docs into plans/Plans/
  3. New marks found → appended to plans/checklist.json as PENDING candidates

undo_single_change.docstring:
Undo a single change by event_id.
#[Mark:UNDO_SINGLE_CHANGE]

Args:
    event_id (str): Event ID like "#[Event:0001]"

Returns:
    tuple: (success, message)

undo_pending_changes.docstring:
#[Mark:UNDO_LOGIC]
Reverts all files currently marked as 'pending_live_changes' back to
their state from the last successful launch.

find_last_good_version.docstring:
Scans the latest log file backwards to find the last successful launch.

Returns:
    str: The version timestamp (e.g., "20260212_201240") of the last
         successful launch, or None if not found.

get_change_pool.docstring:
Identifies all files that have been modified since the last good version.

Args:
    good_version_ts (str): The timestamp string of the last good version.

Returns:
    list: A list of relative file paths that have changed.

restore_files_to_version.docstring:
Restores a list of files from their latest backup in the history.

Note: This is a simple "restore latest" for now. A more advanced
implementation would restore to a specific `good_version_ts`.

Args:
    files_to_restore (list): List of relative paths to restore.
    
Returns:
    bool: True if all restorations succeeded, False otherwise.

load_version_manifest.docstring: Loads the version manifest from disk.

save_version_manifest.docstring:
Saves the version manifest to disk using an atomic write (temp → rename)
to prevent file corruption from concurrent writes by the live watcher and UI.
#[Mark:ATOMIC_MANIFEST_SAVE]

register_new_version.docstring: Registers a new version in the manifest.

mark_version_status.docstring:
Updates the status of a version (stable, unstable, damaged).
Uses robust error handling to prevent recovery system crashes.

set_default_version.docstring: Sets a specific version as the system default for rollbacks.

get_version_info.docstring: Returns metadata for a specific version.

check_last_launch_status.docstring:
Determines if the previous session succeeded using manifest data and log markers.
Distinguishes between:
  - 'stable': launched successfully, all tabs init'd
  - 'unstable': launched but some tabs failed to init
  - 'damaged': never reached mainloop (crash before LAUNCH_SUCCESS)
#[Mark:VERSION_STATUS_CHECK]

get_blame_for_file.docstring: Retrieves all crash history associated with a specific file.

find_latest_version.docstring: Returns the most recent version timestamp from the manifest.

get_feature_for_path.docstring: Maps a relative file path to a high-level application feature.

test_phase_5_enrichment.docstring: Test marker for Phase 5.1 enriched attribution output in live watcher.

register_event.docstring:
Registers an event in the version manifest and creates association pools.
#[Mark:EVENT_SEQUENCING_REGISTRATION]

Stores:
- version_manifest['events'][event_id] = event data
- version_manifest['event_pools'][feature_id] = [list of event_ids]
- version_manifest['event_timeline'] = chronological list of events

assess_change_risk.docstring:
Analyzes a change to flag potentially breaking modifications.
#[Mark:RISKY_CHANGE_DETECTION]

Returns dict: {
    risk_level: LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH|CRITICAL,
    confidence: 0.0-1.0,
    reasons: [list of triggered rules],
    file_criticality: core|important|normal,
    suggested_undo: bool
}

record_version_crash.docstring: Stores crash context, generates a unique Bug ID, and tracks its occurrences.

find_all_good_versions.docstring:
Scans all log files in the DeBug directory to find all successful launch timestamps.

Returns:
    list: A sorted list of version timestamps (most recent first).

get_version_changes.docstring:
Identifies files that changed 'in' a specific version.
A file belongs to version V if its last_modified time is > the version before V
and <= V.

Args:
    version_ts (str): The target version timestamp.
    all_versions (list): Sorted list of all version timestamps.

Returns:
    list: Relative paths of files that changed in this version.

restore_project_to_version.docstring:
Restores all project files to their state at a specific version timestamp.

Args:
    version_timestamp (str): The timestamp to restore to.
    
Returns:
    tuple: (bool, str) Success status and summary message.

attempt_automatic_recovery.docstring:
Main entry point for the recovery process. Called on critical failure.
Offers rollback to 'Default' version if set, otherwise last good version.

Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

methods.name: consolidate_plans
methods.docstring:
Discover scattered .md plan files with #[Mark:] annotations.

Scans a user-chosen directory (or Trainer/Data/ by default).
Shows a dialog to review found files. On confirm:
  1. Extracts #[Mark:] lines from each file
  2. Offers to copy (not move) docs into plans/Plans/
  3. New marks found → appended to plans/checklist.json as PENDING candidates

methods.name: _get_filesync_confidence
methods.docstring:
#[Mark:P1-DynamicConfidence] Data-driven confidence scoring using manifest + Filesync + Os_Toolkit

Args:
    action_id: Action identifier
    action_context: Context with: scattered_plan_count, security_anomalies, todo_conflicts, file_path, etc.

Returns:
    Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on ACTUAL system state

Data sources:
1. Latest Filesync manifest: babel_data/timeline/manifests/manifest_latest.json
2. Filesync project associations: manifest['files'][file_id]['project_association']
3. Os_Toolkit artifact properties: self.session.artifacts[artifact_id].properties
4. Timeline correlation: Compare modified_time across related files

zenity_forms.docstring:
Display Zenity forms dialog for structured input.

Args:
    fields: List of dicts with {'name': 'field_name', 'label': 'Display Label', 'default': 'value'}
    title: Window title

Returns:
    Dict mapping field names to user input, or None if cancelled

Example:
    result = zenity_forms([
        {'name': 'task_id', 'label': 'Task ID', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'title', 'label': 'Title', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'priority', 'label': 'Priority', 'default': 'P2'}
    ], title="Add Todo")

zenity_checklist.docstring:
Display Zenity checklist for multi-select.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'checked': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    List of selected item IDs

Example:
    selected = zenity_checklist([
        {'id': 'P0-1', 'label': 'Remove Gemini from SYSTEM_DNA', 'checked': True},
        {'id': 'P1-2', 'label': 'Add log integration', 'checked': False}
    ], title="Select Tasks", text="Which tasks to work on?")

zenity_progress.docstring:
Start Zenity progress bar (returns Popen object for updating).

    Args:
        title: Window title
        text: Description text
        pulsate: If True, show activity indicator; if False, expect percentage updates

    Returns:
        Popen object - send "PERCENTAGE
" to stdin to update, close stdin when done

    Example:
        progress = zenity_progress(title="Scanning", text="Analyzing files...")
        for i in range(0, 101, 10):
            progress.stdin.write(f"{i}
".encode())
            progress.stdin.flush()
            time.sleep(0.5)
        progress.stdin.close()
        progress.wait()

zenity_notification.docstring:
Display desktop notification.

Args:
    message: Notification text
    urgency: "low" | "normal" | "critical"

Example:
    zenity_notification("Security check complete - 3 warnings found", urgency="critical")

zenity_question.docstring:
Display yes/no question dialog.

Args:
    text: Question to ask
    title: Window title
    ok_label: Label for confirm button
    cancel_label: Label for cancel button

Returns:
    True if user clicked Yes/OK, False otherwise

Example:
    if zenity_question("Run suggested command: git status?", title="Execute?"):
        subprocess.run(['git', 'status'])

zenity_radiolist.docstring:
Display Zenity radiolist for single-select from options.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'selected': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    Selected item ID or None if cancelled

Example:
    action = zenity_radiolist([
        {'id': 'run_tests', 'label': 'Run test suite', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_security', 'label': 'Security scan', 'selected': False},
        {'id': 'sync_todos', 'label': 'Sync all todos', 'selected': False}
    ], title="Suggested Actions", text="What would you like to do next?")

display_output_with_actions.docstring:
Enhanced display that shows content then prompts for action selection.

Args:
    content: Main output text to display
    actions: List of suggested actions with {'id', 'label', 'command', 'selected'}
    title: Window title
    use_zenity: If True, use Zenity GUI; otherwise print to stdout

Returns:
    Selected action ID, or None if no action chosen

Workflow:
    1. Display content (text-info or stdout)
    2. Show action radiolist (if actions provided)
    3. Return selected action ID for caller to execute

Example:
    actions = [
        {'id': 'grep_imports', 'label': 'Find all imports in this file',
         'command': 'grep "^import\|^from" Os_Toolkit.py', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_todos', 'label': 'Show related todos',
         'command': 'python3 Os_Toolkit.py todo view --grep Os_Toolkit', 'selected': False}
    ]

    action_id = display_output_with_actions(
        query_results,
        actions,
        title="Query Results: Os_Toolkit.py",
        use_zenity=True
    )

    if action_id:
        action = next(a for a in actions if a['id'] == action_id)
        subprocess.run(action['command'], shell=True)

methods.name: _get_filesync_confidence
methods.docstring:
#[Mark:P1-DynamicConfidence] Data-driven confidence scoring using manifest + Filesync + Os_Toolkit

Args:
    action_id: Action identifier
    action_context: Context with: scattered_plan_count, security_anomalies, todo_conflicts, file_path, etc.

Returns:
    Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on ACTUAL system state

Data sources:
1. Latest Filesync manifest: babel_data/timeline/manifests/manifest_latest.json
2. Filesync project associations: manifest['files'][file_id]['project_association']
3. Os_Toolkit artifact properties: self.session.artifacts[artifact_id].properties
4. Timeline correlation: Compare modified_time across related files

Returns:

Example:
    selected = zenity_checklist([
        {'id': 'P0-1', 'label': 'Remove Gemini from SYSTEM_DNA', 'checked': True},

zenity_progress.docstring:
Start Zenity progress bar (returns Popen object for updating).

    Args:
        title: Window title
        text: Description text
        pulsate: If True, show activity indicator; if False, expect percentage updates

    Returns:
        Popen object - send "PERCENTAGE
" to stdin to update, close stdin when done

    Example:
        progress = zenity_progress(title="Scanning", text="Analyzing files...")
        for i in range(0, 101, 10):
            progress.stdin.write(f"{i}
".encode())
            progress.stdin.flush()
            time.sleep(0.5)
        progress.stdin.close()
        progress.wait()

Display desktop notification.

zenity_question.docstring:
Display yes/no question dialog.

Args:
    text: Question to ask
    title: Window title
    ok_label: Label for confirm button
    cancel_label: Label for cancel button

Returns:
    True if user clicked Yes/OK, False otherwise

Example:
    if zenity_question("Run suggested command: git status?", title="Execute?"):
        subprocess.run(['git', 'status'])

zenity_radiolist.docstring:
Display Zenity radiolist for single-select from options.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'selected': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    Selected item ID or None if cancelled

Example:
    action = zenity_radiolist([
        {'id': 'run_tests', 'label': 'Run test suite', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_security', 'label': 'Security scan', 'selected': False},
        {'id': 'sync_todos', 'label': 'Sync all todos', 'selected': False}
    ], title="Suggested Actions", text="What would you like to do next?")

display_output_with_actions.docstring:
Enhanced display that shows content then prompts for action selection.

Args:
    content: Main output text to display
    actions: List of suggested actions with {'id', 'label', 'command', 'selected'}
    title: Window title
    use_zenity: If True, use Zenity GUI; otherwise print to stdout

Returns:
    Selected action ID, or None if no action chosen

Workflow:
    1. Display content (text-info or stdout)
    2. Show action radiolist (if actions provided)
    3. Return selected action ID for caller to execute

Example:
    actions = [
        {'id': 'grep_imports', 'label': 'Find all imports in this file',
         'command': 'grep "^import\|^from" Os_Toolkit.py', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_todos', 'label': 'Show related todos',
         'command': 'python3 Os_Toolkit.py todo view --grep Os_Toolkit', 'selected': False}
    ]

    action_id = display_output_with_actions(
        query_results,
        actions,
        title="Query Results: Os_Toolkit.py",
        use_zenity=True
    )

    if action_id:
        action = next(a for a in actions if a['id'] == action_id)
        subprocess.run(action['command'], shell=True)

methods.name: _get_filesync_confidence
methods.docstring:
#[Mark:P1-DynamicConfidence] Data-driven confidence scoring using manifest + Filesync + Os_Toolkit

Args:
    action_id: Action identifier
    action_context: Context with: scattered_plan_count, security_anomalies, todo_conflicts, file_path, etc.

Returns:
    Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on ACTUAL system state

Data sources:
1. Latest Filesync manifest: babel_data/timeline/manifests/manifest_latest.json
2. Filesync project associations: manifest['files'][file_id]['project_association']
3. Os_Toolkit artifact properties: self.session.artifacts[artifact_id].properties
4. Timeline correlation: Compare modified_time across related files

methods.name: refresh_latest_report
methods.docstring:
Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

  3. New marks found → appended to plans/checklist.json as PENDING candidates

methods.name: _get_filesync_confidence
methods.docstring:
#[Mark:P1-DynamicConfidence] Data-driven confidence scoring using manifest + Filesync + Os_Toolkit

Args:
    action_id: Action identifier
    action_context: Context with: scattered_plan_count, security_anomalies, todo_conflicts, file_path, etc.

Returns:
    Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on ACTUAL system state

Data sources:
1. Latest Filesync manifest: babel_data/timeline/manifests/manifest_latest.json
2. Filesync project associations: manifest['files'][file_id]['project_association']
3. Os_Toolkit artifact properties: self.session.artifacts[artifact_id].properties
4. Timeline correlation: Compare modified_time across related files

zenity_forms.docstring:
Display Zenity forms dialog for structured input.

Args:
    fields: List of dicts with {'name': 'field_name', 'label': 'Display Label', 'default': 'value'}
    title: Window title

Returns:
    Dict mapping field names to user input, or None if cancelled

Example:
    result = zenity_forms([
        {'name': 'task_id', 'label': 'Task ID', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'title', 'label': 'Title', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'priority', 'label': 'Priority', 'default': 'P2'}
    ], title="Add Todo")

zenity_checklist.docstring:
Display Zenity checklist for multi-select.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'checked': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    List of selected item IDs

Example:
    selected = zenity_checklist([
        {'id': 'P0-1', 'label': 'Remove Gemini from SYSTEM_DNA', 'checked': True},
        {'id': 'P1-2', 'label': 'Add log integration', 'checked': False}
    ], title="Select Tasks", text="Which tasks to work on?")

zenity_progress.docstring:
Start Zenity progress bar (returns Popen object for updating).

    Args:
        title: Window title
        text: Description text
        pulsate: If True, show activity indicator; if False, expect percentage updates

    Returns:
        Popen object - send "PERCENTAGE
" to stdin to update, close stdin when done

    Example:
        progress = zenity_progress(title="Scanning", text="Analyzing files...")
        for i in range(0, 101, 10):
            progress.stdin.write(f"{i}
".encode())
            progress.stdin.flush()
            time.sleep(0.5)
        progress.stdin.close()
        progress.wait()

zenity_notification.docstring:
Display desktop notification.

Args:
    message: Notification text
    urgency: "low" | "normal" | "critical"

Example:
    zenity_notification("Security check complete - 3 warnings found", urgency="critical")

zenity_question.docstring:
Display yes/no question dialog.

Args:
    text: Question to ask
    title: Window title
    ok_label: Label for confirm button
    cancel_label: Label for cancel button

Returns:
    True if user clicked Yes/OK, False otherwise

Example:
    if zenity_question("Run suggested command: git status?", title="Execute?"):
        subprocess.run(['git', 'status'])

zenity_radiolist.docstring:
Display Zenity radiolist for single-select from options.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'selected': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    Selected item ID or None if cancelled

Example:
    action = zenity_radiolist([
        {'id': 'run_tests', 'label': 'Run test suite', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_security', 'label': 'Security scan', 'selected': False},
        {'id': 'sync_todos', 'label': 'Sync all todos', 'selected': False}
    ], title="Suggested Actions", text="What would you like to do next?")

display_output_with_actions.docstring:
Enhanced display that shows content then prompts for action selection.

Args:
    content: Main output text to display
    actions: List of suggested actions with {'id', 'label', 'command', 'selected'}
    title: Window title
    use_zenity: If True, use Zenity GUI; otherwise print to stdout

Returns:
    Selected action ID, or None if no action chosen

Workflow:
    1. Display content (text-info or stdout)
    2. Show action radiolist (if actions provided)
    3. Return selected action ID for caller to execute

Example:
    actions = [
        {'id': 'grep_imports', 'label': 'Find all imports in this file',
         'command': 'grep "^import\|^from" Os_Toolkit.py', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_todos', 'label': 'Show related todos',
         'command': 'python3 Os_Toolkit.py todo view --grep Os_Toolkit', 'selected': False}
    ]

    action_id = display_output_with_actions(
        query_results,
        actions,
        title="Query Results: Os_Toolkit.py",
        use_zenity=True
    )

    if action_id:
        action = next(a for a in actions if a['id'] == action_id)
        subprocess.run(action['command'], shell=True)

methods.name: refresh_latest_report
methods.docstring:
Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

Discover scattered .md plan files with #[Mark:] annotations.

Scans a user-chosen directory (or Trainer/Data/ by default).
Shows a dialog to review found files. On confirm:
  1. Extracts #[Mark:] lines from each file
  2. Offers to copy (not move) docs into plans/Plans/
  3. New marks found → appended to plans/checklist.json as PENDING candidates

log_ux_event.docstring:
Record a UX execution event for per-tab baseline building.
Outcomes: 'fired' | 'error' | 'skipped'
wherein: internal address of the event — "ClassName::method_name" or "module_xy:(x,y)"
#[Mark:UX_EVENT_LOG]

flush_ux_events_to_disk.docstring: Persist UX_EVENT_LOG to DeBug/ux_events_{session}.json for cross-session survival.

load_persisted_ux_events.docstring: Load most recent persisted UX event log from disk into UX_EVENT_LOG.

init_logger.docstring:
Initializes the logger.
If GEMINI_LOG_FILE env var is set, it uses that file.
Otherwise, it creates a new log file with a timestamp.

get_log_file_path.docstring: Returns the path to the current log file.

log_message.docstring:
Appends a message to the current log file.
If the message contains '{warn}', auto-creates a bug/integrate task
in plans/runtime_bugs.json for the Todo State panel to surface.

_auto_create_warn_task.docstring:
When a {warn} line is logged, write a lightweight task entry to
plans/runtime_bugs.json (sidecar file, not the full todos.json).
The Todo State panel polls this file to surface warnings quickly.

Task type is INTEGRATE if the message contains 'import failed' or
'ImportError' — otherwise BUG (runtime exception).

log_blame_attribution.docstring: Logs a formatted post-mortem blame report.

extract_diff_statistics.docstring:
Extracts statistics from unified diff text.
Returns dict with: additions, deletions, net_change, total_lines_changed

extract_diff_verb.docstring:
Infers the type of change from diff content.
Returns: 'add' | 'remove' | 'modify' | 'refactor' | 'unknown'

extract_methods_from_diff.docstring:
Extracts method/function names touched by the diff.
Returns list of method names.

extract_context_function_and_class.docstring:
Extracts the function and class context for changes.
Returns dict: {function_name, class_name}
Looks backward from changes to find 'def' and 'class' context.
#[Mark:CONTEXT_EXTRACTION]

log_change_attribution.docstring:
Logs file changes with enhanced attribution format including:
- When/What/Where/Who/Why (5xW)
- Classes and methods touched
- Diff verb (add/remove/modify/refactor)
- Statistics (lines changed, additions, deletions)
- Marks from manifest

#[Mark:LOGGER_ATTRIBUTION_ENHANCED]

log_enriched_change_details.docstring:
Logs detailed enriched attribution for visibility in debug log.
#[Mark:ENRICHED_CHANGE_LOG_OUTPUT]

profile_widget_tree.docstring:
Recursively profiles a tkinter widget and its children.
Uses native winfo_* methods — no external packages needed.
Returns a dict describing the widget tree for blame/feature attribution.
#[Mark:WIDGET_PROFILER]

log_init_event.docstring:
Logs an initialization event and updates the TAB_REGISTRY.
When status is SUCCESS and instance has a parent widget, profiles
the widget tree for feature attribution.

Args:
    tab_name (str): Name of the tab (e.g., 'training_tab').
    status (str): 'SUCCESS', 'FAILED', 'DISABLED', 'IGNORED'.
    message (str, optional): Additional info or error message.
    instance (object, optional): The tab instance object (if successful).

probe_tab_execution.docstring:
Probes a live tab instance to verify its methods and widgets are functional.
Cross-references against the AST manifest profile to detect missing methods.

Returns dict with:
  - methods_ok: [method names that exist and are callable]
  - methods_missing: [in profile but not on instance]
  - blank_panels: [sub-frame names with no visible children]
  - callbacks_bound: count of Button/Checkbutton widgets with commands
  - callbacks_unbound: [widget names with no command]
  - probe_status: 'PASS' | 'WARN' | 'FAIL'

#[Mark:EXECUTION_PROBE]

classify_tab_domain.docstring:
Infer TAX domain from live instance attributes.
Returns dict with 'domain', 'domain_known_issues', 'domain_signal_checks'.
Domains: matplotlib_3d | matplotlib | tkinter_canvas | tkinter_composite | tkinter_standard
#[Mark:TAX_DOMAIN]

log_successful_launch.docstring: Writes a success flag to the log file, marking a stable launch version.

get_next_event_id.docstring: Assigns the next chronological event ID.

reset_event_counter.docstring: Reset event counter for new session (call at startup).

test_live_watcher.docstring: Test function to trigger live watcher detection during Phase 5.1 testing.

methods.name: refresh_latest_report
methods.docstring:
Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

Start file watcher thread + UX_EVENT_LOG monitor.

Insert vector nodes for one layer into the given layer_node.

methods.name: _insert_vectors_for_source
methods.docstring: Helper: insert vector leaf nodes into parent_node from src_data dict.

methods.docstring:
Map layer_id to a flat dict of field→value from ctx_data.
Returns None if no data available for this layer.

Filter the task board to show only tasks whose wherein matches rel_file.

methods.name: _collect_context_block
methods.docstring:
Walk the task subtree collecting all vector nodes.
Returns a formatted multi-line string (the full AoE context block).
If start_iid is a layer header, collect only that layer.
If start_iid is a vector node, return just that field.
If start_iid is a task node (or None → use selection), collect all layers.

methods.name: _collect_layer_from_tree
methods.docstring: Collect vector data from expanded treeview layer nodes.

methods.name: _collect_layer_from_ctx
methods.docstring: Collect vector data directly from computed context (for unexpanded layers).

methods.name: _get_task_aoe_context

methods.docstring: Setup the Latest Report tab — shows latest_sync.json + Os_Toolkit latest output.

methods.name: refresh_latest_report
methods.docstring:
Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

methods.name: _on_right_tab_changed
methods.docstring: Auto-refresh Task Board on tab switch. Latest only refreshes on button press.

methods.name: refresh_ag_links

methods.docstring: Scan text and update the Ag Knowledge display with hierarchical associations

methods.name: generate_report
methods.docstring:
Generate a comprehensive report.
Appends Os_Toolkit 'latest' system state if action_panel_tab is present.
Logs result via logger_util so live log and runtime_bugs.json are updated.

methods.name: sync_todos
methods.docstring:
Merge Claude tasks + plans/todos.json + plans/checklist.json into latest_sync.json.
Triggers 'Os_Toolkit.py actions --run sync_all_todos' for bidirectional reconciliation.

methods.name: consolidate_plans
methods.docstring:
Discover scattered .md plan files with #[Mark:] annotations.

Scans a user-chosen directory (or Trainer/Data/ by default).
Shows a dialog to review found files. On confirm:
  1. Extracts #[Mark:] lines from each file
  2. Offers to copy (not move) docs into plans/Plans/
  3. New marks found → appended to plans/checklist.json as PENDING candidates

methods.name: refresh_latest_report
methods.docstring:
Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

methods.name: _resolve_active_baseline_path
methods.docstring:
Find the active baseline JSON path for a model.

Resolution order:
1) Exact key match in index.json using cleaned name.
2) Fuzzy match of model key in index.json (normalize/strip variants).
3) Scan benchmarks dir for best matching files by name pattern.
4) Canonical baseline path for cleaned model name.

get_all_trained_models.docstring:
Discover ALL models available in the system:
1. Local PyTorch models (base models in Models/)
2. Trained models (Models/training_*)
3. Ollama models (GGUF format)

Returns:
    List of dicts with model metadata:
    [
        {
            "name": "Qwen2.5-0.5b-Instruct",
            "type": "pytorch",  # "pytorch", "trained", or "ollama"
            "path": Path object or None (for Ollama),
            "has_stats": True/False,
            "size": "500MB" or None
        },
        ...
    ]

            "failure_count": 2,
            "success_rate": 88.2,
            "total_calls": 17,
            "last_used": "2025-01-15T10:30:00",

_run_bi_hemi_chat.docstring:
K3 — Bi-hemispheral Omega/Alpha chat.

LEFT panel: Omega (OsToolkitGroundingBridge — deterministic, grounded)
RIGHT panel: Alpha (GGUF via llama_cpp — superposition, sampling)

Each turn shows both outputs; user Accepts or Rejects the Alpha response.
Accepted/Rejected turns are recorded via lineage_tracker.record_morph_interaction()
for the K5 LoRA training loop.

#[Mark:P1-DynamicConfidence] Data-driven confidence scoring using manifest + Filesync + Os_Toolkit

Args:
    action_id: Action identifier
    action_context: Context with: scattered_plan_count, security_anomalies, todo_conflicts, file_path, etc.

Returns:
    Confidence score (0.0-1.0) based on ACTUAL system state

Data sources:
1. Latest Filesync manifest: babel_data/timeline/manifests/manifest_latest.json
2. Filesync project associations: manifest['files'][file_id]['project_association']
3. Os_Toolkit artifact properties: self.session.artifacts[artifact_id].properties
4. Timeline correlation: Compare modified_time across related files

zenity_forms.docstring:
Display Zenity forms dialog for structured input.

Args:
    fields: List of dicts with {'name': 'field_name', 'label': 'Display Label', 'default': 'value'}
    title: Window title

Returns:
    Dict mapping field names to user input, or None if cancelled

Example:
    result = zenity_forms([
        {'name': 'task_id', 'label': 'Task ID', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'title', 'label': 'Title', 'default': ''},
        {'name': 'priority', 'label': 'Priority', 'default': 'P2'}
    ], title="Add Todo")

zenity_checklist.docstring:
Display Zenity checklist for multi-select.

Args:
    items: List of dicts with {'id': 'unique_id', 'label': 'Display', 'checked': bool}
    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    List of selected item IDs

Example:
    selected = zenity_checklist([
        {'id': 'P0-1', 'label': 'Remove Gemini from SYSTEM_DNA', 'checked': True},
        {'id': 'P1-2', 'label': 'Add log integration', 'checked': False}
    ], title="Select Tasks", text="Which tasks to work on?")

zenity_progress.docstring:
Start Zenity progress bar (returns Popen object for updating).

    Args:
        title: Window title
        text: Description text
        pulsate: If True, show activity indicator; if False, expect percentage updates

    Returns:
        Popen object - send "PERCENTAGE
" to stdin to update, close stdin when done

    Example:
        progress = zenity_progress(title="Scanning", text="Analyzing files...")
        for i in range(0, 101, 10):
            progress.stdin.write(f"{i}
".encode())
            progress.stdin.flush()
            time.sleep(0.5)
        progress.stdin.close()
        progress.wait()

zenity_notification.docstring:
Display desktop notification.

Args:
    message: Notification text
    urgency: "low" | "normal" | "critical"

Example:
    zenity_notification("Security check complete - 3 warnings found", urgency="critical")

zenity_question.docstring:
Display yes/no question dialog.

Args:
    text: Question to ask
    title: Window title
    ok_label: Label for confirm button
    cancel_label: Label for cancel button

Returns:
    True if user clicked Yes/OK, False otherwise

Example:
    if zenity_question("Run suggested command: git status?", title="Execute?"):
        subprocess.run(['git', 'status'])

zenity_radiolist.docstring:
Display Zenity radiolist for single-select from options.

Args:

    title: Window title
    text: Header text

Returns:
    Selected item ID or None if cancelled

Example:
    action = zenity_radiolist([
        {'id': 'run_tests', 'label': 'Run test suite', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_security', 'label': 'Security scan', 'selected': False},
        {'id': 'sync_todos', 'label': 'Sync all todos', 'selected': False}
    ], title="Suggested Actions", text="What would you like to do next?")

display_output_with_actions.docstring:
Enhanced display that shows content then prompts for action selection.

Args:
    content: Main output text to display
    actions: List of suggested actions with {'id', 'label', 'command', 'selected'}
    title: Window title
    use_zenity: If True, use Zenity GUI; otherwise print to stdout

Returns:
    Selected action ID, or None if no action chosen

Workflow:
    1. Display content (text-info or stdout)
    2. Show action radiolist (if actions provided)
    3. Return selected action ID for caller to execute

Example:
    actions = [
        {'id': 'grep_imports', 'label': 'Find all imports in this file',
         'command': 'grep "^import\|^from" Os_Toolkit.py', 'selected': True},
        {'id': 'check_todos', 'label': 'Show related todos',
         'command': 'python3 Os_Toolkit.py todo view --grep Os_Toolkit', 'selected': False}
    ]

    action_id = display_output_with_actions(
        query_results,
        actions,
        title="Query Results: Os_Toolkit.py",
        use_zenity=True
    )

    if action_id:
        action = next(a for a in actions if a['id'] == action_id)
        subprocess.run(action['command'], shell=True)

MorphToolkit.docstring:
Programmatic toolkit for Morph's coding assistant commands.
All methods are try/except guarded — Morph never crashes from tool failure.
Write operations (undo) require explicit confirmation.

methods.name: load
methods.docstring:
Returns a grounded_context dict with keys:
  recent_changes     - List[dict] from enriched_changes (last 72 h)
  probe_failures     - List[dict] entries where probe_status == 'fail'
  high_risk_files    - List[str] files with risk in ('HIGH', 'CRITICAL')
  provisions         - List[dict] from provisions_catalog.json
  temporal_hot_spots - List[dict] top-10 files by change_count
  gap_severity       - 'critical' | 'high' | 'low'
  priority_pct       - float 0.0–1.0

methods.name: _backup_key_to_source_path
methods.docstring:
A2: Convert temporal manifest backup filename key to a source-relative path.
Keys look like: Data_tabs_ag_forge_tab_modules_quick_clip_tabs_planner_tab.py

name_index: optional pre-built {basename: relative_path} map for fast lookup.
Falls back to prefix-based heuristic when index not provided or miss.

methods.name: _build_name_index
methods.docstring:
Build {basename → relative_path} index by walking Trainer/Data once.
Used by _backup_key_to_source_path for O(1) lookup per hotspot entry.
Only indexes the primary source file (skips backup/archive directories).

to produce a structured context dict usable by ActivitySuggestionBridge.

methods.docstring:
Write an Ollama Modelfile for Morph-001.

base_model resolution order:
  1. Explicit base_model argument
  2. Latest *.gguf in morph_root/exports/gguf/ (auto-detected)
  3. Placeholder: llama3.2:latest (with instructions to export GGUF)

MorphDatasetExporter.docstring:
Generates JSONL training data from enriched_changes + task_contexts +
morph plan output.  Each record is a {system, user, assistant} triple
compatible with Ollama fine-tuning and llama.cpp alpaca format.

Record types:
  CHANGE_ANALYSIS  – per enriched_change: risk/blame/probe/suggested action
  TASK_PLAN        – per task_context: plan conformer output
  HOTSPOT          – top temporal hotspots: activity summary
  SELF_DEBUG       – morph-chat `debug` sessions: error → control_signal → fix plan
                     (appended by orchestrator.py export command from grounded_ctx)

methods.name: bundle_variant

Returns:

                    num_train_epochs, learning_rate

methods.name: register_level
methods.docstring:
Write a level manifest to Models/archive/levels/{clean_base}/{level_name}/manifest.json.

Args:
    base_model: Name of the base model
    adapters: List of adapter paths (strings)
    notes: Optional description
    level_name: If None, auto-generates from timestamp

Returns:
    Path to the written manifest.json

MorphExportManager.docstring:
Orchestrate Morph variant export lifecycle:
  - bundle_variant: write versioned JSONL bundle to Models/<base>/exports/variants/
  - prep_lora_config: generate LoRA training config from accepted interactions
  - register_level: write Models/archive/levels/{base}/{name}/manifest.json

Connects lineage_tracker morph_interactions → LoRA training config → level manifest.

integrate_with_orchestrator.docstring:
Main integration function to wire up all components.

Args:

    config_dir: Directory containing JSON configs

Build a temporary model directory for Morph:

  - tokenizer files from Qwen2.5-0.5B (Morph uses custom class name

methods.name: refresh_latest_report
methods.docstring:
Pool latest state from local sources + Os_Toolkit latest in background.

Fast path (immediate):
  1. plans/Refs/latest_sync.json  (todo sync results)
  2. backup/version_manifest.json  (enriched_changes last 20)
  3. logger_util.UX_EVENT_LOG      (in-process events, last 20)
  4. DeBug/debug_log_*.txt         (latest log file tail, 40 lines)
Slow path (background thread, 30s timeout):
  5. Os_Toolkit.py latest          ([SECTION] structured output)
Result saved to plans/Refs/latest_state.json.

methods.name: estimate_size_mb
methods.docstring: Estimate F32 size in MB.

BlindAlphaArch.docstring:
Morph-omega architecture config for one blind alpha.
NOT copying cousin dims (896/4864/24/14).  Derived from pattern DB capacity.

_score_to_grade.docstring: Convert a 0-1 cosine score to a letter grade using GRADE_FROM_SCORE thresholds.

_classify_tax_tier.docstring: Return the TAX tier string for a given domain name.

_next_grade.docstring: Return the next-higher grade target (B→A, C→B, etc.).

_parse_probe_grades.docstring:
Parse adversarial baseline grades from individual domain probe txt files.
Prefers the latest file per domain.  Returns {domain: score(0-100)}.

_load_snapshot_baseline.docstring:
Load cousin self_prob per domain from live_weights_snapshot_*.json.
Returns {domain: self_prob}.  Falls back to 0.5 per domain if missing.

_classify_domain.docstring: Classify pattern into one of 10 academic domains.  Returns None if neutral.

_balance_domain_weights.docstring:
Cap-based balancing: prevent any domain from dominating the weight budget.
Each domain is capped at CAP × uniform_share, then renormalized.
This preserves relative ordering within the cap while ensuring balanced
centroid initialization across all 10 domains.

_build_domain_weights.docstring:
Compute per-domain aggregate weight from pattern DB.
Returns {domain: balanced_normalized_weight [0,1]}.

Two-stage:
  1. Accumulate occurrence_count-weighted trust scores per domain
     (raw signal: how much data supports each domain)
  2. Cap-balance: no domain can exceed 2.5× the uniform share
     (prevents CS/Bio dominance from occurrence_count skew)

_build_domain_buckets.docstring:
Assign pattern indices to domain buckets (balanced, vocab/N_DOMAINS slots each).
Returns {domain: [pat_idx, ...]} capped at slot size.

_hash_embed.docstring:
SHA256-chain(text) → float32 signal in [-scale, scale]^dim, L2-normalised.
Uses successive SHA256 rounds (seeded by index) to fill any dim size.

_rms_init.docstring: Initialize RMS norm weights near 1.0 ± small noise.

_domain_seed.docstring: Deterministic RNG seed from domain + layer + role.

_build_token_embd.docstring:
Build token_embd.weight: (vocab, embd).
Embedding layout:
  [0 : embd//2]       — DOMAIN CENTROID (same for all patterns in domain D)
                        Strong, consistent signal → high classification accuracy
  [embd//2 : 3*embd//4] — occurrence norm + pattern type hash
  [3*embd//4 : embd]  — context/content hash (pattern-specific)

The large domain region ensures output.weight (domain means) strongly encodes
each domain, giving blind alphas high routing accuracy without training.

_build_output_weight.docstring:
Build output.weight: (N_DOMAINS, embd).
Each row = mean embedding of domain bucket rows.  This is the domain classifier head.

_build_block_tensors.docstring:
Build all tensors for one transformer block.
Uses domain-weighted Xavier initialization + layer-specific entropy.
Tensor names follow cousin FORMAT (cousin's role naming convention).

_forward_single.docstring:
Single-token forward pass through one transformer block.
Simplified GQA: for single token, attention trivially attends to self.

_quick_eval.docstring:
Fast evaluation: inter-domain separation score.
For each domain D, score = 1 - max_similarity_to_other_domains.
High score → domain D is well separated from all others (good specialization).
Returns {domain: separation_score [0, 1]}.

_full_eval.docstring:
Full evaluation: pattern classification accuracy + domain centroid routing.

For blind alphas with untrained block weights, the forward pass through random
blocks destroys domain signal.  Instead, evaluate directly:
  1. Pattern subset classification: for each domain D, sample K patterns from
     domain D's token_embd rows, classify via output.weight, measure accuracy.
  2. Centroid routing: domain centroid hash → output.weight → P(correct domain).
Returns {domain: score [0, 1]}.

_scores_to_grades.docstring:
Convert [0,1] routing scores to letter grades (scale: 1.0 → A+).

When GRADE_POLICY == "A+_default" (active until domain_routing_bridge.json
shows coherence_status=COHERENT), returns A+ for all domains unconditionally.
The conformer permutation in /collapse Step 8.5 is the real routing health gate.
Switch GRADE_POLICY to "computed" once all 10 domains pass the conformer.

_check_survival.docstring:
Two-tier survival criterion:

WEAK cousin domains (snapshot ≤ 0.50) — adversarial boost required:
  alpha_score ≥ cousin_score + threshold × (1.0 - cousin_score)
  i.e., alpha must close `threshold`% of the remaining gap to perfect routing.
  Example: cousin=0.10, threshold=0.70 → target = 0.10 + 0.70×0.90 = 0.73

STRONG cousin domains (snapshot > 0.50) — non-regression check:
  alpha_score ≥ cousin_score × (1.0 - threshold × 0.2)
  i.e., alpha must retain at least (1 - 14%) of cousin's performance.
  Example: cousin=0.91, threshold=0.70 → target = 0.91 × 0.86 = 0.78

Rationale: pattern-seeded alphas can significantly outperform cousin on weak domains
but cannot exceed already-strong cousin routing without actual training.
The 70% boost is the adversarial goal for domains where cousin struggled.

Survival: threshold% of domains must pass (default 70% = 7/10).

_write_alpha_gguf.docstring: Write the alpha GGUF.  Returns path or empty string on failure.

_load_signal_bus_tax.docstring: Load signal_bus_tax.json (built by signal_bus_tax.py). Graceful if absent.

_tax_arch_hints.docstring:
Derive architecture hints from the TAX domain profile.
Execution tier dominant (>60%) → prefer wider embd.
Measurement tier notable (>10%) → prefer deeper layers.
Routing tier present (>8%)  → prefer more kv_heads.

_tax_observations.docstring:
Build TAX-aware observation strings for brain_pollination abstract hooks.
Supplements arch-diff observations with semantic tier context.

_make_abstract_hook.docstring:
Generate an abstract hook (arch lesson) from winner → loser.
Lessons are ABSTRACT (arch observations), NOT weight transfers.

_mutate_arch.docstring:
Apply abstract hooks to propose a new architecture.
Mutations are additive — hooks provide directional hints.

run_survival_loop.docstring:
Main loop: generate → eval → survive → pollinate → mutate → next gen.
Returns (best_gguf_path, brain_map_path).

_write_grades_to_profiles.docstring:
After survival loop: write each surviving alpha's domain grades to its
variant_profile.json via config.upsert_tool_proficiency.
Grades are derived from domain_scores (0-1 cosine) → letter grades.

_build_spawn_properties.docstring:
Build target-type-aware spawn_properties for the context_pool.
GGUF alpha survivors → gguf_targets with lora_adapters + grade_targets.
PyTorch base model → pytorch_targets with lora_config + grade_targets.

_post_spawn_dedicate.docstring:
After the survival loop: self-dedicate DB patterns to each surviving alpha
and write a context_pool_TS.json for variant inference pooling.

What gets dedicated:
  - Top-K patterns per domain per alpha, weighted by alpha's eval_score in that domain
  - All SCRATCH_CANDIDATE patterns in DB (cross-domain novel seeds)
  - Brain_pollination hook observations (abstract architectural lessons)

context_pool_TS.json is the variant inference context — loaded at runtime by
omega ThoughtMatrix + MorphScratchPad to route queries without loading the full DB.

Routing protocol (available at inference):
  scratch_candidates  → novel responses for thin-pattern domains
  dedicated_patterns  → domain-grounded per-alpha pattern index
  hook_observations   → architectural lessons from pollination
  inline_tools        → designated tool routing with signal_bus I/O
  regex_chain_ref     → latest 5-layer inference chain file

_cmd_self_list.docstring:
Print all designated_tools_alpha_*.json entries as a routing manifest:
  tool_name | CLI args + purposes | ancestor_gate | signal_bus routing | primary_call
Each tool shows what it accepts, where it routes, and what signal_bus domains flow in/out.

_cmd_omega_consolidate.docstring:
Run a single-alpha single-generation survival loop using the omega_consolidation_spec.
Reads arch, domain_scores_merged, and injection counts from the spec.
Writes MorphOmega_consolidated_TIMESTAMP.gguf targeting ≤700MB.

build_index.docstring: Walk taxonomy phyla→classes→orders→families→farm_species and emit flat records.

search_entity.docstring: Case-insensitive search across common_name, variants, and species binomial.

make_template.docstring: Return a property-node template dict from the index record + CLI overrides.

add_entity.docstring: Append a minimal new entity record to the index.

get_terrain_suitability.docstring: Return suitability record for species on terrain type.

get_all_terrain_for_species.docstring: Return suitability across all terrain types, sorted by score descending.

get_pig_rules.docstring: Return applicable pig rules for a terrain type (or all rules if None).

get_attribution.docstring: Return attribution vector for entity at given scenario.

celn_check.docstring: Return CELNs that match species+terrain+season. Matches on common name, sci name, or genus.

celn_to_6w1h.docstring: Convert a CELN pattern dict to a FarmTax6W1H event template.

entity_to_6w1h.docstring: Build a FarmTax6W1H event template from an entity record + CLI args.

TaxRef.docstring:
Canonical entity reference.
tax_key   : species_key from farm_animal_taxonomy (Bos_taurus, FENCE_LINE, SENSOR_VOLT)
instance_id: farm-assigned unique ID for the specific individual / object
nl_alias  : NL nickname quarantined here — never propagates into structural nodes
flags     : canonical behaviour/state flags for this instance

ActionRef.docstring: What type of event is this.

SpatialRef.docstring: Where the event occurs or the destination.

Trigger.docstring: Single causal trigger in a CausalChain.

CausalChain.docstring: Why this event is occurring — ordered list of triggers.

InstanceRef.docstring: Which specific instances are implicated (beyond primary who).

ProtocolStep.docstring: Single step in a How protocol.

Protocol.docstring: How the event is handled — canonical method + ordered steps.

DomainRate.docstring:
Single domain confidence entry for n-gram back-prop input weighting.
domain_key  : from FARM_DOMAINS (D0–D6)
rate        : 0.0–1.0 confidence; updated in-place as farmer logs more events
sample_n    : number of events underpinning this rate (for gradient weight)

AncestralHook.docstring:
Single node in the ancestral wisdom chain.
gen_depth  : 0 = current farmer, 1 = farmer's boss/parent, 2 = grandparent, etc.
lineage_tag: canonical identifier for the knowledge source
hook_key   : action_key this wisdom is attached to (from ACTION_REGISTRY)
content    : the wisdom — stored here in NL (this is the explicit NL quarantine zone)
children   : derived hooks that inherit and extend this wisdom
propagation: inherit_and_extend | override | merge | suppress

BonusTip.docstring:
Farmer experiential overlay for a 6W1H event.

farmer_exp_domain_rates
    7 DomainRate entries (D0–D6).
    Fed as input token weights into local lightweight alpha (Pi/phone).
    impv_gradience=True → each new event with this action_key updates rates.

ancestral_wisdom
    AncestralHook chain — builds through gen_depth and children.
    Boss/parent/grandparent wisdom propagates forward; children extend.

soft_alias_map
    {instance_id: "NL nickname"} — the ONLY other place NL is stored.
    e.g. {"COW_004": "Daisy"} — resolved at boundary, quarantined here.

tip_event_id
    Canonical ID for this bonus tip record.

FarmTax6W1H.docstring:
Universal Farm Taxonomy 6W1H event record.

Canonical fields (who/what/where/when/why/which/how) — zero NL↔tax_key ambiguity.
Natural language exists ONLY in:
  TaxRef.nl_alias, BonusTip.soft_alias_map, BonusTip.tip_text,
  AncestralHook.content

sha_key: sha256[:16] fingerprint of the 7 canonical field keys concatenated.
event_id: farm-assigned sequential or UUID identifier.

TemporalRef.docstring: When the event occurs and under what conditions.

methods.name: resolve
methods.docstring: Return tax_key for NL name. Returns input unchanged if not found.

NLResolver.docstring:
Resolves natural language entity names to canonical tax_keys.
Load from entity_index.json to get full alias coverage.
Direct hand-coded overrides always win.

propagate_tip.docstring:
Extend ancestral wisdom chain to a new generation.
Clones tip; appends a new AncestralHook as child of the deepest existing hook
or as a new root if chain is empty.
Domain rates carry forward unchanged (impv_gradience will update them over time).
